key_facts: The 2026 Louisiana Senate race is for the seat currently held by Republican Bill Cassidy.
Louisiana uses a jungle primary (nonpartisan blanket primary) system where all candidates run on one ballot regardless of party.
If no candidate receives over 50% in the jungle primary, the top two advance to a runoff.
A GOP-vs-GOP runoff is plausible given Louisiana's strong Republican lean.
No web research tools were available to retrieve current 2026 debate schedule or announcement information.
Louisiana's federal jungle primary is typically held in the fall with a potential December runoff.
